articles.title: Gold eases as fresh US-Iran tensions lift oil prices, rate-hike bets
articles.description: Gold eased on Wednesday to its lowest level in nearly a week, as U.S. strikes on Iran boosted oil prices and the dollar, raising concerns that ​inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer and weigh on non-yielding ‌bullion.

articles.title: Global Market Today: Oil rises, Asian stocks steady as US strikes Iran
articles.description: Oil prices surged as the United States conducted airstrikes and revoked waivers for Iran. Asian stocks remained largely unchanged while investors assessed the geopolitical situation. S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures showed slight gains after earlier declines in US markets. Renewed tensions risk energy market disruptions and undermine the recent US-Iran peace deal. Investors are now shifting focus from AI sentiment to geopolitical concerns.

articles.title: PetChem leads FBM KLCI higher as Hormuz attacks ignite oil supply concerns
articles.description: KUALA LUMPUR: A jump in crude oil prices following Iran's attack on tankers in the Straits of Hormuz is spurring activity in the energy sector once again, even as investors rotate out of the overheated AI-drive technology sector.

articles.title: Asian shares steady, oil prices rise as US strikes Iran
articles.description: South Korea's Kospi index was up 0.9%, while Japan's Nikkei index was flat but sharply off day's lows. The MSCI Asia Pacific index was steady after falling nearly 1% during open. S&P 500 futures were up0.1% while contracts for the Nasdaq 100 were up 0.3%.

articles.title: Govt to conduct extensive E25 testing before rollout decision
articles.description: India News: New Delhi: Govt will carry out extensive testing of E25 (petrol blended with 25% ethanol), evaluate reports and consult all stakeholders before taking.

articles.title: US reinstates sanctions on Iranian oil sales after LNG, oil tanker attacks
articles.description: United States reimposed sanctions on Iranian oil sales Tuesday. This action followed attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Treasury had previously authorized sales until August 21. Tuesday's revocation shortens this wind-down period to July 17. Oil prices rose more than five percent after the announcement.

articles.title: Oil prices jump more than 3% after US strikes on Iran, while shares in Asia are mixed
articles.description: Asian shares are mixed and oil prices have surged more than 3% after the U.S. launched strikes on Iran. BANGKOK (AP) — Asian shares were mixed on

articles.title: Dollar at week-high after US resumes attacks on Iran
articles.description: The dollar reached its highest weekly level against peers amid renewed U.S. strikes on Iran. Geopolitical tensions escalated, and oil prices saw an increase following the attacks. Concerns over peace deal stability reemerged after Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz. Global yields jumped higher as inflation outlook worries intensified for investors. Other currencies like the euro and pound experienced slight declines against the dollar.

articles.title: US oil prices jump after US military launches strikes against Iran
articles.description: U.S. oil prices increased significantly after military strikes against Iran. These actions followed Iranian attacks on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. A Qatari liquefied natural gas tanker reported being struck by a drone. Maritime security sources indicated a Saudi-flagged crude oil tanker was also damaged. Fears of disruptions to tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz have resurfaced.

articles.title: Brent, WTI rise as U.S. targets Iran
articles.description: Oil prices rose on Wednesday after the U.S. launched fresh strikes on Iran in retaliation for attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
